binaries.title: kbdhid.sys - No security-relevant change: KbdHid_Create (IRP_MJ_CREATE) device-name string corrected from 'KBD' to '\KBD', metadata opens newly allowed

changed_functions.name: KbdHid_Create
key_changes: String comparison target changed from 'KBD' (0x440042004b, 3 UTF-16 chars, Length=6 bytes) to '\KBD' (leading backslash, 4 UTF-16 chars, Length=8 bytes)
RtlEqualUnicodeString CaseInSensitive parameter changed from FALSE (0, case-sensitive) to TRUE (1, case-insensitive)
NEW DesiredAccess test added: 'test r13b, r12b; jz 0x1C0004E17' at new basic block 0x1C0004E05, checking if FILE_READ_DATA (bit 0) is set in DesiredAccess

New control flow: when FileName matches '\KBD' and DesiredAccess does NOT have FILE_READ_DATA set, the create is ALLOWED to proceed to normal processing (0x1C0004E17). In the unpatched build every non-empty relative name was denied, so this is a newly permitted path (a relaxation), not a newly added denial.
Register allocation restructured: r12 now holds DesiredAccess (was r13 in unpatched), r13 holds constant 1 (was r12 in unpatched)
Stack frame increased from 0xa0 to 0xb0 bytes to accommodate the larger '\KBD' string
changed_functions.security_notes: KbdHid_Create is the IRP_MJ_CREATE dispatch handler for kbdhid.sys. When a create carries a non-empty relative FileName it is compared against a hardcoded constant. UNPATCHED: FileName=='KBD' (exact, case-sensitive) returns STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION; anything else returns STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED. Every non-empty relative name is therefore denied; because the real relative name is '\KBD' (with a backslash), the case-sensitive 'KBD' constant never matches it and such opens hit STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED. PATCHED: FileName=='\KBD' (case-insensitive) with FILE_READ_DATA set returns STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION; '\KBD' without FILE_READ_DATA is allowed through; anything else returns STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED. Direction: the patch does NOT add a bypassable authorization check. The unpatched build is the stricter one for named opens (it denies all of them); the patch introduces a NEW allowed path for attribute-only '\KBD' opens. Read-data opens of '\KBD' are denied in both builds. The security-sensitive operation (connecting the keyboard for keystroke reading via KbdHid_StartRead) is reached only from the empty-name path guarded by 'test cl, 1' (FILE_READ_DATA), which is byte-identical in both builds. No keystroke-read authorization is added, removed, or changed. This is a functional correction of a device-name string plus a benign metadata-open relaxation, not a security fix. No CWE applies.

security_findings.entry_point: IRP_MJ_CREATE dispatch handler (DriverObject->MajorFunction[IRP_MJ_CREATE], set in DriverEntry @ 0x1C000D008)
call_chain: CreateFile/NtCreateFile with a non-empty relative name on the kbdhid device
I/O Manager dispatches IRP_MJ_CREATE to kbdhid.sys
KbdHid_Create @ 0x1C0004CB0 - compares FileObject->FileName against the hardcoded constant
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (functional device-name string correction; benign metadata-open relaxation)
security_findings.description: KbdHid_Create is the IRP_MJ_CREATE dispatch handler. When a create carries a non-empty relative FileName, the handler compares it against a hardcoded constant and returns an error status. UNPATCHED: FileName=='KBD' (exact, case-sensitive, Length=6, 0x440042004b) returns STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION (0xc0000043); anything else returns STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED (0xc0000022). Every non-empty relative name is denied; the case-sensitive 'KBD' constant never matches the real relative name '\KBD' (with backslash), so '\KBD' opens hit STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED. PATCHED: the constant is corrected to '\KBD' (Length=8) with case-insensitive matching, and a DesiredAccess test is added ('test r13b, r12b; jz 0x1C0004E17' at 0x1C0004E05, where r13=1 and r12=DesiredAccess read from IO_SECURITY_CONTEXT+0x10). A '\KBD' open with FILE_READ_DATA returns STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION; a '\KBD' open without FILE_READ_DATA is allowed to proceed to normal processing; anything else returns STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED. DIRECTION: the patch does not add a bypassable authorization check. The unpatched build is stricter for named opens (all denied); the patch introduces a NEW allowed path for attribute-only '\KBD' opens (a relaxation). Read-data opens of '\KBD' are denied in both builds. The keystroke-read connect path (KbdHid_StartRead) is reached only from the empty-name path guarded by 'test cl, 1' (FILE_READ_DATA), which is byte-identical in both builds. No keystroke-read authorization is added, removed, or changed. No CWE applies.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: The name-check branch is entered only when the IRP_MJ_CREATE carries a FileObject (IO_STACK_LOCATION+0x30) that is non-NULL and whose FileName (FILE_OBJECT+0x58) length is non-zero, i.e. a relative open with a name component. RtlEqualUnicodeString does full-length equality, so the compared constant matches only an exact 'KBD' (unpatched) or '\KBD' (patched). An empty-name open bypasses the branch entirely in both builds and goes straight to normal processing. Observable difference: an attribute-only open (DesiredAccess without FILE_READ_DATA) of the relative name '\KBD' returns STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED on the unpatched build and succeeds on the patched build. Any open of '\KBD' that requests FILE_READ_DATA is denied on both builds (STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED unpatched via name mismatch, STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION patched).
security_findings.primitive: None. The change grants no additional access to the keyboard input stream. Reading keystrokes requires FILE_READ_DATA and goes through KbdHid_StartRead on the empty-name path, whose guard is identical in both builds; named '\KBD' read-data opens are denied in both builds. The only behavioral change is that attribute-only '\KBD' opens, previously denied, now succeed - which yields no keystroke access and no additional capability.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED KbdHid_Create @ 0x1C0004CB0 - IRP_MJ_CREATE handler (name-check region)
0x1C0004D5F: mov     rcx, [r14+30h]           ; FileObject
0x1C0004D63: test    rcx, rcx
0x1C0004D66: jz      loc_1C0004E01            ; NULL -> normal processing
0x1C0004D6C: add     rcx, 58h                 ; &FileObject->FileName
0x1C0004D70: cmp     [rcx], r15w              ; FileName.Length == 0 ?
0x1C0004D74: jz      loc_1C0004E01            ; empty -> normal processing
0x1C0004D7A: mov     rax, 440042004Bh         ; 'KBD' UTF-16LE (no backslash)
0x1C0004D84: mov     dword ptr [rsp+String2.Length], 80006h  ; Length=6, MaxLength=8
0x1C0004D8C: mov     [rsp+var_48], rax
0x1C0004D94: lea     rdx, [rsp+String2]
0x1C0004DA1: xor     r8d, r8d                 ; CaseInSensitive = FALSE
0x1C0004DA4: mov     [rsp+String2.Buffer], rax
0x1C0004DA9: call    cs:__imp_RtlEqualUnicodeString
0x1C0004DB5: mov     rcx, [r14+30h]
0x1C0004DB9: test    al, al
0x1C0004DBB: jnz     short loc_1C0004DF4      ; match ('KBD') -> SHARING_VIOLATION
0x1C0004DBD: mov     esi, 0C0000022h          ; STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED (no match)
0x1C0004DC2: mov     r9d, 0Ch
0x1C0004DC8: ...                              ; log + complete IRP with error
0x1C0004DF4: mov     esi, 0C0000043h          ; STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION (match)
0x1C0004DF9: mov     r9d, 0Dh
0x1C0004DFF: jmp     short loc_1C0004DC8
0x1C0004E01: lea     rcx, [rdi+98h]           ; normal processing (IoAcquireRemoveLockEx follows)

; PATCHED KbdHid_Create @ 0x1C0004CB0 (name-check region with added DesiredAccess test)
0x1C0004D62: mov     rax, [r14+30h]           ; FileObject
0x1C0004D66: test    rax, rax
0x1C0004D69: jz      loc_1C0004E17            ; NULL -> normal processing
0x1C0004D6F: lea     rcx, [rax+58h]           ; &FileObject->FileName
0x1C0004D73: cmp     [rcx], r15w              ; FileName.Length == 0 ?
0x1C0004D77: jz      loc_1C0004E17            ; empty -> normal processing
0x1C0004D7D: movzx   eax, word ptr cs:aKbd+8
0x1C0004D89: movsd   xmm0, qword ptr cs:aKbd  ; '\KBD' (with backslash)
0x1C0004D91: mov     r8b, r13b                ; CaseInSensitive = TRUE (r13 = 1)
0x1C0004DB2: mov     dword ptr [rsp+String2.Length], 0A0008h  ; Length=8, MaxLength=0xA
0x1C0004DBA: call    cs:__imp_RtlEqualUnicodeString
0x1C0004DC6: test    al, al
0x1C0004DC8: jnz     short loc_1C0004E05      ; match ('\KBD') -> DesiredAccess test
0x1C0004DCA: mov     esi, 0C0000022h          ; STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED (no match)
0x1C0004E05: test    r13b, r12b               ; r13=1, r12=DesiredAccess -> FILE_READ_DATA bit
0x1C0004E08: jz      short loc_1C0004E17      ; no read data -> ALLOWED (normal processing)
0x1C0004E0A: mov     esi, 0C0000043h          ; STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION (read data requested)
0x1C0004E0F: mov     r9d, 0Dh
0x1C0004E15: jmp     short loc_1C0004DD5      ; log + complete IRP with error
0x1C0004E17: lea     rcx, [rdi+98h]           ; normal processing (IoAcquireRemoveLockEx follows)

breakpoints.why: IO_STACK_LOCATION field reads. r14 = IO_STACK_LOCATION, rbx = IRP. r14+0x08 = IO_SECURITY_CONTEXT pointer, r14+0x1a = ShareAccess word, r14+0x30 = FileObject pointer; SecurityContext+0x10 = DesiredAccess.

Name comparison setup. Unpatched loads the constant 'KBD' (0x440042004b, Length=6) with CaseInSensitive=0 (xor r8d,r8d). Patched loads '\KBD' from cs:aKbd (Length=8) with CaseInSensitive=1 (r8b=r13b=1). The constant differs (missing vs present backslash) and the case sensitivity differs.

test al,al after RtlEqualUnicodeString. AL=1 means the name matched. Unpatched: match -> 0x1C0004DF4 SHARING_VIOLATION, no-match -> 0x1C0004DBD ACCESS_DENIED (both deny). Patched: on match, the added 'test r13b, r12b; jz 0x1C0004E17' at 0x1C0004E05 checks DesiredAccess&FILE_READ_DATA before deciding.

Normal processing (IoAcquireRemoveLockEx). Reached in the unpatched build only when FileObject is NULL or FileName.Length is 0. In the patched build it is ALSO reached when the name matches '\KBD' and DesiredAccess bit 0 (FILE_READ_DATA) is clear.

watch: [r14+0x30] = FileObject pointer (the file object being created)
r13 = 1 (patched, constant used for test r13b,r12b)
al = RtlEqualUnicodeString result after call at 0x1c0004da4
key_offsets: 0x1C0004D7A (unpatched): mov rax, 0x440042004b - loads 'KBD' (case-sensitive constant, no backslash); patched loads '\KBD' from cs:aKbd at 0x1C0004D89
0x1C0004DA1 (unpatched): xor r8d, r8d - CaseInSensitive=FALSE; patched sets r8b=r13b=1 (TRUE) at 0x1C0004D91
0x1C0004DB9 (unpatched): test al, al; jnz 0x1C0004DF4 - name match check with no DesiredAccess test; patched adds 'test r13b, r12b; jz 0x1C0004E17' at 0x1C0004E05
Patched new block 0x1C0004E05: test r13b, r12b; jz 0x1C0004E17 - DesiredAccess&FILE_READ_DATA test that ALLOWS read-less '\KBD' opens through
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Issue a relative-name open on the kbdhid device from user mode (a create whose FileObject->FileName is non-empty). 2. The open generates an IRP_MJ_CREATE IRP dispatched to KbdHid_Create @ 0x1C0004CB0. 3. Break at the name comparison (0x1C0004D7A unpatched / 0x1C0004D7D patched). 4. Unpatched compares the exact 'KBD' (case-sensitive); patched compares '\KBD' (case-insensitive). 5. To exercise the added DesiredAccess test (patched only): open the name '\KBD' with DesiredAccess that does NOT include FILE_READ_DATA (0x1), e.g. FILE_READ_ATTRIBUTES (0x80).
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Unpatched: for a FileName '\KBD', RtlEqualUnicodeString returns AL=0 ('KBD' != '\KBD'), so STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED. For a FileName exactly 'KBD' (no backslash), AL=1 and STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION. Every non-empty relative name is denied. Patched: FileName '\KBD' without FILE_READ_DATA -> the test at 0x1C0004E05 jumps to 0x1C0004E17 (normal processing), so the open proceeds; FileName '\KBD' with FILE_READ_DATA -> STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION; any other name -> STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED. Read-data opens of '\KBD' are denied in both builds; only attribute-only '\KBD' opens change (denied -> allowed).
debugging_aids.notes: The changed logic is in KbdHid_Create @ 0x1C0004CB0, the IRP_MJ_CREATE handler, called by the I/O Manager on device opens. DeviceExtension is at DeviceObject+0x40. The keyboard read-connect path (KbdHid_StartRead @ 0x1C000393C) is reached only from the empty-name normal-processing path when DesiredAccess has FILE_READ_DATA set and no keyboard is already connected; that guard ('test cl, 1') is byte-identical in both builds, so this diff does not affect keystroke-read authorization. DesiredAccess is at IO_SECURITY_CONTEXT+0x10; ShareAccess at IO_STACK_LOCATION+0x1a. RtlEqualUnicodeString does exact-length matching, so 'KBD' (Length=6) matches only a 3-char 'KBD', never '\KBD' (4 chars). The driver is kbdhid.sys (PDB 'kbdhid.pdb', provider 'Microsoft.Windows.Input.KbdHid').

summary: The only function body changed between the two builds is KbdHid_Create @ 0x1C0004CB0 (the IRP_MJ_CREATE dispatch handler); every other function is byte-identical apart from relocations. The change corrects the hardcoded relative-open name compared by RtlEqualUnicodeString from 'KBD' (case-sensitive, no backslash) to '\KBD' (case-insensitive, with backslash) and adds a DesiredAccess test (test r13b, r12b; jz 0x1C0004E17 at 0x1C0004E05). In the unpatched build every non-empty relative name is denied (STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION for an exact 'KBD', STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED otherwise). In the patched build a '\KBD' open without FILE_READ_DATA is allowed to proceed, while '\KBD' with FILE_READ_DATA still returns STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION and other names still return STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED. This is a direction the report must state carefully: the patch does not add a bypassable authorization check - it is a functional correction of a device-name string plus a benign relaxation that newly permits attribute-only '\KBD' opens. Read-data opens of '\KBD' are denied in both builds, and the keystroke-read connect path (KbdHid_StartRead, empty-name path guarded by 'test cl, 1') is byte-identical in both builds. No CWE applies; this is not a security-relevant change.
